Product: Synteromycin 250 mg/5 mL oral suspension (synteromycin); strength 250 mg/5 mL; route oral.
Applicant: Horizon Regulatory Services Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: BlueRidge Formulations Ltd (South Africa).
PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=oral suspension; Strength=250 mg/5 mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to the active ingredient or related medicines. Seek immediate medical care for rash, breathing difficulty, or severe diarrhoea. Use exactly as prescribed; complete the full treatment course where applicable.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

SmPC for dossier HUM-NEW-INNOV-AMR-SYNT-0001.
Product: Synteromycin 250 mg/5 mL oral suspension (synteromycin); strength 250 mg/5 mL; route oral.
Applicant: Horizon Regulatory Services Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: BlueRidge Formulations Ltd (South Africa).
SmPC 4.1 Therapeutic indications: As approved in the application form.
SmPC 4.2 Posology and method of administration: Dose by age/weight and clinical condition as approved.
SmPC 4.3 Contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to active ingredient or excipients, Severe allergy to related therapeutic class where applicable.
SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately.

PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

Product: Multivitamin Blend label claim oral solution (multivitamin blend); strength label claim; route oral.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=oral solution; Strength=label claim.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, goats, sheep only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

Product: Antiinflamox 50 mg/mL injectable suspension (antiinflamox); strength 50 mg/mL; route subcutaneous.
Applicant: MediAxis Healthcare (EA) Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: GrandOak Therapeutics Ltd (Egypt).
PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=subcutaneous; Dosage form=injectable suspension; Strength=50 mg/mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, dogs only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.

Product: Multivitamin Blend label claim oral solution (multivitamin blend); strength label claim; route oral.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=oral solution; Strength=label claim.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, goats, sheep only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

SmPC 4.3 Contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to active ingredient or excipients, Severe allergy to related therapeutic class where applicable.
SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.

PIL Route and Form: Route=intramammary; Dosage form=intramammary infusion; Strength=100 mg/syringe.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to the active ingredient or related medicines. Seek immediate medical care for rash, breathing difficulty, or severe diarrhoea. Use exactly as prescribed; complete the full treatment course where applicable. For veterinary use in dairy cattle only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

Product: Ceftriaxone 1 g powder for injection (ceftriaxone); strength 1 g; route intravenous.
Applicant: Crestline Medicines Agency Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: BlueRidge Biopharma Ltd (India).
PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=intravenous or intramuscular; Dosage form=powder for injection; Strength=1 g.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Reference-aligned warning: hypersensitivity to cephalosporins. Reference-aligned warning: serious allergic reactions. Reference-aligned warning: antibiotic-associated diarrhoea.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C and protect from light.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.
Antimicrobial Classification: Watch; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

SmPC for dossier HUM-REN-GEN-AMR-CEFT-0033.
Product: Ceftriaxone 1 g powder for injection (ceftriaxone); strength 1 g; route intravenous.
Applicant: Crestline Medicines Agency Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: BlueRidge Biopharma Ltd (India).
SmPC 4.1 Therapeutic indications: As approved in the application form.
SmPC 4.2 Posology and method of administration: Dose by age/weight and clinical condition as approved.
SmPC 4.3 Contraindications: hypersensitivity to ceftriaxone or other cephalosporins.
SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: serious hypersensitivity reactions; antibiotic-associated colitis.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.
Antimicrobial Classification: Watch; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

Patient Information Leaflet for dossier VET-REN-BIO-NONAMR-ANTI-0035.
Product: Antiinflamox 50 mg/mL injectable suspension (antiinflamox); strength 50 mg/mL; route subcutaneous.
Applicant: Horizon Healthcare (EA) Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: PrimeCrest Healthcare Ltd (South Africa).
PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=subcutaneous; Dosage form=injectable suspension; Strength=50 mg/mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, dogs only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

SmPC for dossier VET-REN-BIO-NONAMR-ANTI-0035.
Product: Antiinflamox 50 mg/mL injectable suspension (antiinflamox); strength 50 mg/mL; route subcutaneous.
Applicant: Horizon Healthcare (EA) Ltd (Rwanda); Manufacturer: PrimeCrest Healthcare Ltd (South Africa).
SmPC 4.1 Therapeutic indications: As approved in the application form.
SmPC 4.2 Posology and method of administration: Dose by age/weight and clinical condition as approved.
SmPC 4.3 Contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to active ingredient or excipients, Severe allergy to related therapeutic class where applicable.
SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.

Product: Antiinflamox 50 mg/mL injectable suspension (antiinflamox); strength 50 mg/mL; route subcutaneous.

Product: Cardiovex 20 mg tablet (cardiovex); strength 20 mg; route oral.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=tablet; Strength=20 mg.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately.

PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.

PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, dogs only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=oral suspension; Strength=250 mg/5 mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to the active ingredient or related medicines. Seek immediate medical care for rash, breathing difficulty, or severe diarrhoea. Use exactly as prescribed; complete the full treatment course where applicable.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.

Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=intramammary; Dosage form=intramammary infusion; Strength=100 mg/syringe.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to the active ingredient or related medicines. Seek immediate medical care for rash, breathing difficulty, or severe diarrhoea. Use exactly as prescribed; complete the full treatment course where applicable. For veterinary use in dairy cattle only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, goats, sheep only.

PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=subcutaneous; Dosage form=injectable suspension; Strength=50 mg/mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, dogs only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.

PIL Route and Form: Route=subcutaneous; Dosage form=injectable suspension; Strength=50 mg/mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, dogs only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=intramammary; Dosage form=intramammary infusion; Strength=100 mg/syringe.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to the active ingredient or related medicines. Seek immediate medical care for rash, breathing difficulty, or severe diarrhoea. Use exactly as prescribed; complete the full treatment course where applicable. For veterinary use in dairy cattle only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.
Antimicrobial Classification: Reserve; stewardship warning and prudent-use statement included.

PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=oral solution; Strength=label claim.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, goats, sheep only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

Product: Antiinflamox 50 mg/mL injectable suspension (antiinflamox); strength 50 mg/mL; route subcutaneous.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=subcutaneous; Dosage form=injectable suspension; Strength=50 mg/mL.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, dogs only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

PIL Section: What this medicine is and what it is used for.
PIL Route and Form: Route=oral; Dosage form=oral solution; Strength=label claim.
PIL Instructions: Follow the prescribed dose schedule. Do not exceed recommended dose.
PIL Warnings: Do not stop treatment abruptly unless advised by a healthcare professional. Tell your doctor about kidney, liver, or heart conditions before use. Report dizziness, fainting, severe abdominal pain, or persistent vomiting immediately. For veterinary use in cattle, goats, sheep only.
PIL Storage: Store below 30C, protect from moisture/light, keep out of reach of children.
PIL Adverse Effects: Common effects include gastrointestinal upset and headache; serious effects require urgent review.

SmPC 4.1 Therapeutic indications: As approved in the application form.
SmPC 4.2 Posology and method of administration: Dose by age/weight and clinical condition as approved.
SmPC 4.3 Contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to active ingredient or excipients, Severe allergy to related therapeutic class where applicable.
SmPC 4.4 Special warnings and precautions: monitor organ function and hypersensitivity risks.
SmPC 4.8 Undesirable effects: nausea, diarrhoea, rash, dizziness; serious adverse reactions listed.
SmPC 6.4 Special precautions for storage: store according to label and stability commitments.
